signals.title: NZD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + Carry + Risk-Off
signals.text: NZD is the weakest G10 currency this week, with NZD 1W avg -1.296% (n=4 pairs) vs USD 1W avg +0.729% (n=7 pairs), creating a significant 2.025% leader-to-laggard spread. This divergence is driven by broad USD strength and risk aversion, with NZD/USD at 0.5660. The 150bp Fed-RBNZ carry premium further favors USD. Elevated VIX at 19.5 (normal volatility) and S&P 500 down -1.44% suggest a risk-off tilt, exacerbating NZD weakness. Watch for RBNZ's next meeting on 8 Jul for any hawkish shift to counter the current trend.

signals.title: USD/JPY — BoJ Summary + COT vs Options Divergence + Carry
signals.text: USD/JPY at 161.56 continues to push higher despite BoJ's June summary revealing broad support for rate hikes and a sharp deflation warning. This confirms the BoJ's hawkish bias (BoJ 1.0% HIKING). However, JPY COT LF net short -112,092 (long%=29.7%) indicates extreme speculative short positioning, while the 25d RR at -1.52 shows strong put skew, signaling options market hedging for JPY strength. This creates a significant divergence between speculative futures positioning and options market sentiment, with both contradicting the BoJ's hawkish stance. The 263bp Fed-BoJ carry premium continues to support USD/JPY, but intervention risk rises on a sustained break above 160.00. The next BoJ meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 31 Jul is key for policy clarity.

signals.title: AUD/USD — CPI Risk + CESI + Volatility
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.6913 is under pressure as Australia's CPI is set to accelerate in May, despite earlier hopes for inflation relief. The AUD has tumbled over 1.20% today due to risk aversion and Fed rate hike bets. AUD CESI net -8 misses indicates data consistently disappointing expectations, reinforcing AUD weakness. The options market is pricing elevated volatility, with AUD/USD IV 20.2% vs HV30 8.3%, resulting in a significant +11.9% VRP, suggesting strong hedging demand for downside protection. This combination of negative data surprises, high vol premium, and risk-off sentiment points to continued AUD vulnerability. The upcoming Australian CPI release is the immediate catalyst.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + Retail Sentiment
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.4209 is showing a significant correlation break with WTI Oil, with corr=-0.601 vs 252d norm=+0.036 (z=-2.17σ). This suggests CAD is decoupling from its traditional oil price sensitivity, likely due to broader USD strength and the 131bp Fed-BoC carry premium favoring USD. Retail sentiment is extremely short USD/CAD at 95% short, presenting a contrarian reversal risk if the pair extends higher, potentially triggering a retail capitulation. Watch for WTI Oil price action to confirm if the correlation break persists.
